questions.question: Explain the basic model for network security and identify its main components.
keyPoints: Sender: Originator of message who wants to send data securely
Message Source: Generates the information/content to be transmitted
Encryption/Decryption Algorithm: Transforms plaintext to ciphertext and back
Keys: Secret values used for encryption and decryption
Secure Channel: Protected communication path between sender and receiver
Receiver: Intended destination who receives and decrypts the message
Adversary: Unauthorized entity attempting to compromise security

questions.question: Differentiate between passive and active attacks in network security with examples.
keyPoints: Passive attacks: Observe/monitor without modifying data; hard to detect
Active attacks: Modify data or disrupt systems; easier to detect
Examples of passive: Eavesdropping, traffic analysis
Examples of active: Masquerade, replay, modification, DoS
Passive attacks threaten confidentiality; active attacks threaten integrity and availability

questions.question: What is the CIA triad? Explain each component with its significance in network security.
keyPoints: Confidentiality: Ensuring only authorized parties can access data; achieved through encryption
Integrity: Ensuring data remains unaltered during transmission; achieved through hash functions/digital signatures
Availability: Ensuring authorized users can access data when needed; achieved through redundancy/protection against DoS
All three are interdependent; compromise of one affects overall security

questions.question: List and briefly explain four types of active attacks in network security.
keyPoints: Masquerade: Attacker pretends to be a different legitimate entity
Replay: Attacker captures valid data and retransmits it to deceive the system
Modification of Messages: Attacker alters message content during transmission
Denial of Service (DoS): Attacker disrupts normal network functionality, preventing service to legitimate users

questions.question: What are security services and security mechanisms? Give examples of each.
keyPoints: Security Services: Specific protective functions provided to counteract security threats
Examples: Authentication, Confidentiality, Integrity, Non-repudiation, Access Control
Security Mechanisms: Methods/tools used to implement security services
Examples: Encryption, Digital Signatures, Access Control Lists, Hash Functions, Authentication Protocols

questions.question: Explain the role of Trusted Third Party (TTP) in the network security model.
keyPoints: TTP assists in secure communication between parties who don't share keys
Key Distribution Center (KDC) is a type of TTP that distributes session keys
Certificate Authorities (CA) issue digital certificates for authentication
TTPs help establish trust without prior communication between parties
Examples: Kerberos, PKI Certificate Authorities

questions.question: What is non-repudiation? How is it achieved in network security?
keyPoints: Non-repudiation ensures sender cannot deny sending a message and receiver cannot deny receiving it
Achieved through digital signatures using asymmetric cryptography
The sender signs the message with their private key; receiver verifies with sender's public key
Provides proof of origin and proof of delivery
Essential for legal and financial transactions

questions.question: With a neat diagram, explain the basic network security model and discuss how security is achieved during message transmission. Include the roles of all entities involved.
keyPoints: Diagram: Sender → [Message] → Encryption → [Ciphertext] → Secure Channel → Decryption → Receiver
Components: Message Source, Sender, Encryption Algorithm, Keys, Secure Channel, Decryption Algorithm, Receiver, Adversary
Security achieved through: Encryption (confidentiality), Key distribution (security), Secure channel (protection)
Adversary attempts: Interception (passive), Modification (active)
Trusted Third Party facilitates key exchange
Algorithm provides transformation, Keys provide secrecy

questions.question: Compare and contrast passive and active attacks in network security. Discuss the CIA triad and explain which security services protect against each type of attack.
keyPoints: Passive: Observe without modifying (eavesdropping, traffic analysis); hard to detect; threaten confidentiality
Active: Modify data or disrupt service (masquerade, replay, modification, DoS); easier to detect; threaten integrity/availability
CIA Triad: Confidentiality, Integrity, Availability
Passive attacks → Protected by confidentiality services (encryption)
Active attacks → Protected by integrity services (hash, digital signatures), authentication, access control
DoS → Protected by availability services (redundancy, firewalls)

questions.question: A banking organization needs to secure its online transaction system. Using the network security model, explain what security services and mechanisms they should implement to protect against: (a) Eavesdropping, (b) Message modification, (c) Impersonation of users, (d) Denial of service attacks.
keyPoints: (a) Eavesdropping → Confidentiality through encryption (AES/TLS)
(b) Message modification → Integrity through hash functions/digital signatures
(c) Impersonation → Authentication through passwords, tokens, biometrics
(d) DoS → Availability through redundancy, firewalls, rate limiting
Additional: Access control, non-repudiation for financial transactions
Use of TTP/Certificate Authority for key management and user authentication
